evidence: Media gallery behavior is spread across multiple modules with shared mutable coordination primitives: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` wires shot selection, notifier navigation, modal state, and context side effects, while `useMobileInteractions` requires external timeout/tap refs from callers and `ShotActions` consumes four separate state/callback bags.

`LightboxShell` depends directly on tasks-pane state (`effectiveTasksPaneOpen`, `effectiveTasksPaneWidth`, `isTasksPaneLocked`, `needsTasksPaneOffset`) and hard-coded z-index contract comments, creating direct coupling between media overlay and layout/pane subsystems.

Boundary ownership is blurred by transitional and facade layers (`handleError` as compatibility wrapper, multiple one-line UI/mobile re-export wrappers), increasing import-path indirection without reducing cross-feature coordination complexity.

`ProjectProvider` composes auth, user settings, selection, CRUD, and defaulting/sync effects in one provider, with bidirectional callback contracts between selection and CRUD hooks (`onProjectsLoaded`, `onProjectCreated`, `onProjectDeleted`, `applyCrossDeviceSync`), which makes ownership harder to explain as one coherent role.

Gallery/lightbox modules show a repeated 'orchestration shell' pattern (`LightboxShell`, `useMediaGalleryHandlers`, `ShotActions`) where each unit owns partial workflow state and behavior, requiring large prop/hook contracts to preserve flow instead of a single feature boundary.

Public API shape includes compatibility/delegation wrappers (`handleError`, UI re-export files, mobile index re-export) while feature modules still depend on deep internals, signaling unresolved architectural layering rather than a clean, small top-level surface.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts defines two public input contracts (`UseSegmentSlotModeInput` and `UseSegmentSlotModeLegacyProps`) and immediately normalizes one into the other (lines 52-156, 489-490), adding a compatibility abstraction layer without domain behavior.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts uses a wide options bag plus nested `data` bag (lines 20-42, 198-220), creating broad pass-through surfaces and hidden coupling to many collaborators.

src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts imports a large set of internals from `@/shared/components/MediaLightbox/*` (hooks, context types, utils; lines 16-33), tightly coupling the edit-images tool to another feature's internals.

src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx composes MediaLightbox-specific components and context (`MediaDisplayWithCanvas`, `EditModePanel`, `ImageEditProvider`; lines 3-12), so MediaLightbox changes can break edit-images even when tool behavior is unchanged.

Join-clips generation has split defaults across seam boundaries: UI path passes `settings.phaseConfig ?? DEFAULT_JOIN_CLIPS_PHASE_CONFIG` (src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:430), while task creation path falls back to `buildJoinClipsPhaseConfig(...)` (src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:293).

The two fallback paths encode different behavior (e.g., `steps_per_phase: [2,2,5]` and extra default LoRA in `buildJoinClipsPhaseConfig`, src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:138-190), so output depends on which boundary populated `phase_config`.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/videoEnhance.ts` defines broad param interfaces (`FilmInterpolationApiParams`, `FlashVsrUpscaleApiParams`) but `buildVideoEnhancePayload` serializes only a subset, creating dead/ignored knobs in the same module.
In `src/shared/lib/tasks/legacy/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts`, helper `hasLegacyVideoEditFields` is unused while adjacent helpers are active, indicating drifted validation logic and local noise.
Multiple files use verbose explanatory scaffolding around simple operations (e.g., constant exports and one-branch helpers), reducing signal-to-noise in routine maintenance.
README has no explicit verification gate section (no required CI/local commands for contract checks), despite governance-contract positioning.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` contains behaviorful contract branches (lazy init detection + dev-only logging) but no adjacent test reference in this module; failures here affect a 158-importer surface.
High-fan-in compatibility wrappers (`handleError.ts`, `button.tsx`, `cn.ts`) rely on transitive tests of downstream modules rather than explicit contract tests at the public facade seams.

`src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts` documents `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` as returning a `{ settings, hasShotSettings }` wrapper, but the function signature returns `OperationResult<SettingsFetchResult>` and all call sites must branch on `ok`.

`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/referenceUpload/referenceDomainService.ts` uses throw/catch+fallback patterns (`throw new Error(...)`, broad `catch { return fallbackUrl; }`) instead of the `OperationResult` envelope used by adjacent service APIs (`toolSettingsService`, `clipInitService`, `generateVideoService`).
`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts` mixes two incompatible public response styles in one boundary module (`EdgeRuntimeResult` object with `errorResponse` vs `OperationResult`-based helpers).

Comment density is substantially above codebase baseline in multiple small utilities (e.g., `src/shared/lib/timelineNormalization.ts` is mostly header text for a single constant; `src/shared/lib/storageKeys.ts` and `src/shared/lib/taskCreation/arrayUtils.ts` contain long narrative comments around straightforward logic).

`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts` contains long tutorial-style comment blocks and repeated 'new unified format' framing that restates interfaces rather than capturing non-obvious constraints.
`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` keeps zero-behavior wrapper exports (`handleError`, `handleAndRethrow`) that only forward to canonical functions, extending alias surface area.

Legacy travel structure fields are still part of mainstream type surfaces: `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput.structure_videos` uses `LegacyStructureVideoConfig[]` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts`, and `StructureVideoConfigWithMetadata` extends `LegacyStructureVideoConfig`.
`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` defines deprecation owner/remove-by metadata, but this module has no enforcement path analogous to runtime removal checks.

Migration governance is inconsistent across similar shims: `src/shared/lib/tasks/legacy/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` and `src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts` actively enforce/report post-removal usage, while travel legacy shape remains type-level accepted without comparable guardrails.

`src/shared/hooks/` is a flat high-volume directory (78 files) with mixed concerns and broad coupling (`imports_from_dirs`: `src/shared/lib/` 50, `src/integrations/supabase/` 43, `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/` 35), indicating weak package boundaries.

`src/shared/lib/` is also a flat high-volume directory (70 files) containing unrelated utilities (`dragDrop.ts`, `dragDropUpload.ts`, `dummyNames.ts`) instead of domain- or capability-scoped subpackages.
`src/shared/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts` is only a re-export shim to `src/shared/settings/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts`, leaving duplicate surface area under two package roots.

`src/shared/lib/dragDropUpload.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/useFileDragTracking.ts` expose the same drop helper API shape (`preventDefaultDragOver`, `createSingleFileDropHandler`), showing cross-directory duplication instead of single ownership.

`handleError` is named like a side-effect-only sink but its contract returns `AppError` (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts:18-20`), while sibling APIs (`reportRecoverableError`) treat it as fire-and-forget (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/recoverableError.ts:16-22`).
Callers mix usage patterns (`throw handleError(...)` in `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts:250-253`), which indicates naming is not clearly signaling required usage.
naming_quality.issues_preventing_higher_score: Error-reporting API names do not consistently communicate whether return values must be propagated, which encourages mixed call patterns.

evidence: `buildInlineEditStateModels` immediately calls itself with the same input (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:260-277`), creating a non-terminating path if invoked.

The current hook tests only assert top-level returned sections (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/__tests__/useInlineEditState.test.ts:223-236`) and do not guard against dead recursive helpers surviving refactors.

`GenerationCreationOutcome` exposes `generation: unknown` (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:39-41`) even though internal logic dereferences `existingGeneration.id` (`:308`, `:321`, `:332`).

`handleRegeneration` accepts `existingGeneration: unknown` (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:301`) but uses object properties without narrowing, bypassing static guarantees in a critical completion path.

`useAdjacentPagePreloader` documents that queue cleanup is intentionally not done on unmount (`src/features/gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts:92-93`), but the scheduler it calls always clears the shared queue both at start and cleanup (`src/features/gallery/lib/adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.ts:101`, `:123-126`).
This makes the preloading service contract inconsistent: hook-level semantics imply shared ownership, coordinator semantics enforce exclusive ownership.
`DataFreshnessManager` creates and exports a singleton at import time and mutates `window` in module scope (`src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts:343-348`).
`getSupabaseClient` silently bootstraps runtime state when startup init was skipped (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts:20-23`), making behavior depend on call order rather than explicit bootstrap.
initialization_coupling.issues_preventing_higher_score: Core runtime services still rely on implicit import/access side effects instead of explicit app bootstrap boundaries.

findings.summary: Media gallery flow is implemented as a distributed state machine across hooks/components.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` accepts a broad orchestration contract (shot arrays, navigation, filter mutators, modal setters, lightbox setter, optional associated-shot flow) and performs both command handling and effectful context reconciliation.

`useMobileInteractions` requires caller-owned mutable refs (`lastTouchTimeRef`, `lastTappedImageIdRef`, `doubleTapTimeoutRef`) to coordinate tap semantics, indicating shared state ownership across module boundaries.

`ShotActions` consumes four grouped prop objects (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`) and still performs control-flow decisions (navigate vs add) using cross-cutting flags, showing state/decision logic split across layers.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a feature-level gallery interaction controller (single hook/service) that owns tap/selection/add-to-shot workflow state and exposes a narrow command/query API to UI components; move mutable refs and transition logic inside that controller, and make presentational components receive only derived view models plus intent callbacks.

findings.summary: Lightbox shell is tightly coupled to tasks-pane layout internals and global layering contract.
evidence: `LightboxShell` directly models tasks-pane internals via multiple props (`effectiveTasksPaneOpen`, `effectiveTasksPaneWidth`, `isTasksPaneLocked`, `needsTasksPaneOffset`) and comments about pane control z-index hierarchy.

The same pane/lightbox state relationship appears in layout behavior tests (`useBottomOffset.test.ts` mocks pane lock/open state plus lightbox-open state), indicating cross-subsystem knowledge requirements rather than a single boundary contract.
findings.suggestion: Define a dedicated layout contract for overlays (e.g., `OverlayViewportConstraints`) produced by layout subsystem hooks, and make lightbox consume that contract only; encapsulate pane/open/lock mechanics and z-index assumptions inside layout modules so media modules stop depending on pane semantics.

findings.summary: Compatibility and pass-through facade layers blur canonical ownership of public APIs.
evidence: `handleError.ts` is explicitly a backward-compatibility wrapper that forwards to newer reporters while advising callers to import elsewhere, signaling an unresolved migration path in the active surface.
Multiple UI modules are one-line re-export façades to deeper `theme/primitives/runtime` modules, and mobile exports similarly re-forward from `index.ts`, increasing API indirection layers.
This pattern creates many nominal entrypoints without adding behavior or strong boundary rules, making it harder to identify the true canonical module for each concern.
findings.suggestion: Publish and enforce a single canonical import surface per concern (error reporting, UI primitives, mobile signals), then complete migration by deprecating/removing compatibility wrappers in phases; add lint/import rules to prevent new deep-or-legacy import paths from reappearing.

findings.summary: Segment-slot hook keeps two public contracts and normalizes one into the other, adding non-functional abstraction overhead.
evidence: useSegmentSlotMode declares both `UseSegmentSlotModeInput` and `UseSegmentSlotModeLegacyProps`, then converts new shape back to legacy in `normalizeUseSegmentSlotModeProps` before real work begins (lines 52-156, 489-490).

Callback hook mirrors this pattern with a broad top-level options object plus nested `data` bag and many extracted members (lines 20-42, 198-220), increasing indirection and making call sites harder to reason about.
findings.suggestion: Pick one canonical input contract for `useSegmentSlotMode` and remove the legacy-normalization path; split callback responsibilities into smaller focused hooks (`segmentDelete`, `shotTransfer`, `reorder`) with explicit narrow inputs.

findings.summary: Edit-images tool directly depends on MediaLightbox internals, creating cross-feature blast radius.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` imports many MediaLightbox internals (hooks/context/utils) rather than a stable tool-agnostic service boundary (lines 16-33).
`InlineEditView` renders MediaLightbox component primitives and provider directly (lines 3-12), so a refactor inside MediaLightbox can regress edit-images without API-level changes.
findings.suggestion: Extract an `imageEdit` domain module with stable interfaces (state machine + actions + shared primitives) and have both MediaLightbox and edit-images depend on that module instead of each other’s internals.

findings.summary: Join-clips phase config defaults are split across UI and task-builder seams, producing path-dependent behavior.
evidence: UI passes `settings.phaseConfig ?? DEFAULT_JOIN_CLIPS_PHASE_CONFIG` into generation flow (JoinClipsPage.tsx:430).

Task builder independently applies fallback `params.phase_config || buildJoinClipsPhaseConfig(params.loras || [])` (joinClips.ts:293), where `buildJoinClipsPhaseConfig` has hardcoded defaults including `[2,2,5]` steps and a baked-in extra LoRA (lines 138-190).
findings.suggestion: Centralize join-clips phase-config default construction into one shared function used by both UI initialization and payload building; remove one fallback path and keep only compatibility migration code behind explicit version checks.

findings.summary: Adjacent thumbnail derivation performs repeated linear scans in render-time model assembly.
evidence: `buildAdjacentVideoThumbnails` loops and calls `segmentSlots.find(...)` for each index in both directions (useSegmentSlotMode.ts:190-203), creating O(n^2) lookup behavior.
This is invoked from `useSegmentSlotModeData` while building lightbox model state (useSegmentSlotMode.ts:339-377), which is used by shot image manager flows that can carry many slots.
findings.suggestion: Precompute a slot-index map (`Map<number, SegmentSlot>`) once per render cycle and use O(1) lookups in adjacent thumbnail calculation and other repeated searches.

findings.summary: Service-layer APIs in the same workflow mix `OperationResult`, thrown exceptions, and fallback-return contracts, forcing callers to handle incompatible error shapes.
evidence: `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` returns `Promise<OperationResult<SettingsFetchResult>>` and encodes failures with `operationFailure`.
`referenceDomainService` throws raw `Error` in `uploadAndProcessReference` but swallows all errors in `resolveReferenceThumbnailUrl` by returning `fallbackUrl` with no structured error signal.
`generateVideoService` and `clipInitService` expose `OperationResult` contracts, while `edgeRequest` also exposes non-OperationResult `EdgeRuntimeResult` for runtime initialization.
findings.suggestion: Standardize boundary APIs on one contract (preferably `OperationResult`) for this layer, including a migration adapter for legacy throw-based functions; then enforce the contract in callers and tests.

findings.summary: Final-step completion checks are not run-scoped, unlike sibling segment discovery, creating a seam inconsistency that can gate on stale rows.
evidence: `findSiblingSegments` first calls `lookupTasksByRunIdWithFallback(...runId...)` and only falls back to orchestrator-id lookup.

`checkFinalStitchStatus` always calls `lookupTasksByOrchestratorIdWithFallback` and never takes `orchestratorRunId`, so final-step state can be derived from a different run when historical rows exist.
findings.suggestion: Pass run id through final-step status checks and use the same run-first/fallback strategy as sibling segment lookup; add ordering/selection rules when multiple candidate final-step rows are returned.

findings.summary: `toCompletionError` returns pre-existing `CompletionError` unchanged, so outer boundary code/context metadata is silently dropped.
evidence: In `toCompletionError`, `if (error instanceof CompletionError) return error;` bypasses new `code/context/message/metadata` passed by outer callers.

`checkOrchestratorCompletion` catch block supplies boundary-specific `code: 'orchestrator_completion_failed'`, `context`, and metadata, but these are ignored whenever an inner function already threw `CompletionError`.
findings.suggestion: Wrap existing `CompletionError` with boundary context (or merge/append metadata) instead of returning it verbatim; preserve original error as `cause` while promoting boundary-level code/context.

findings.summary: Several critical boundary/util modules in this batch have no focused tests, leaving failure-path behavior unverified.
evidence: No direct test module surfaced for `referenceDomainService`, despite multi-step upload/transform/session/storage behavior and mixed throw/fallback logic.
No direct tests surfaced for `edgeOperation`, even though it defines the shared error/success envelope consumed by edge request, billing, and rate limit paths.
No direct tests surfaced for date-bound policy logic in `deprecationPolicy`, wrapper behavior in `recoverableError`, and wrapping semantics in `complete_task/errors`.
findings.suggestion: Add targeted unit tests for each listed boundary module, prioritizing failure-path contracts (invalid payloads, auth/session absence, date boundary behavior, and error-wrapping semantics) rather than only consumer-level integration tests.

findings.summary: Travel-between-images input types still embed deprecated structure-video fields in the primary read/write surface.
evidence: `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` defines `structure_videos?: LegacyStructureVideoConfig[]` even though comments state write path should emit canonical shape.
`StructureVideoConfigWithMetadata` extends `LegacyStructureVideoConfig`, which propagates deprecated keys into common UI-facing types.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a strict canonical type (`StructureVideoConfig[]`) for all new write paths and UI state, and confine legacy fields to a dedicated adapter-only input type used at parse boundaries.

findings.summary: Travel legacy deprecation policy is documented but not actively enforced, unlike sibling migration adapters.
evidence: `legacyStructureVideo.ts` exports owner/remove-by constants from policy but performs no usage checks, warnings, or removal-target enforcement.
`joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` uses `isPastRemovalTarget` to throw when legacy fields persist past target.
`toolConstants.ts` calls `signalPastRemovalTargetUsage` to enforce/report deprecated alias usage.
findings.suggestion: Add a travel-structure adapter that records legacy-field usage and enforces removal targets (warn/throw) consistently with join-clips and tool-constants migration policy.

findings.summary: Video-enhance parameter interfaces expose options that payload construction does not serialize, creating misleading local API complexity.
evidence: `FilmInterpolationApiParams` includes fields such as `fps` and `video_write_mode`, but `buildVideoEnhancePayload` omits them from `payload.interpolation`.
`FlashVsrUpscaleApiParams` includes `output_format`, `output_write_mode`, and `preserve_audio`, but `buildVideoEnhancePayload` only forwards a small subset.
findings.suggestion: Either fully map declared fields into payload serialization with validation, or shrink interfaces to the true supported subset to keep module contracts honest and compact.

findings.summary: Several small utility modules are dominated by restating comments and tutorial narration, reducing maintainability signal.
evidence: `timelineNormalization.ts` contains a long block comment for one constant export.
`storageKeys.ts` and `arrayUtils.ts` include extensive descriptive prose for direct, self-evident key builders and array transforms.
`travelBetweenImages/types.ts` repeatedly narrates 'new unified format' and examples inline, creating high non-behavioral churn in a migration-heavy file.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to non-obvious rationale/constraints only; move long migration guides/examples to dedicated docs and keep type files focused on contracts.

findings.summary: A legacy shim keeps `useDebouncedSettingsSave` exposed from `shared/hooks` even though ownership moved to `shared/settings/hooks`, creating duplicate package entry points.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts:1` is a pure re-export to `@/shared/settings/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave`.

The assigned test imports from `../useDebouncedSettingsSave` (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebouncedSettingsSave.test.ts:25`), which reinforces old package placement instead of the settings-owned package boundary.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1) make `src/shared/settings/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts` the only canonical export and add/verify a barrel there; Stage 2) move `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebouncedSettingsSave.test.ts` under `src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/` and switch imports to settings paths; Stage 3) remove `src/shared/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts` shim after callers are migrated. Import update and validation commands: `rg -n "@/shared/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave|../useDebouncedSettingsSave" src`, apply path updates, then run `npx vitest src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/useDebouncedSettingsSave.test.ts` and `npx tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` combines domain mutation orchestration with UI dialog props, weakening layer boundaries for a shared hook package.
evidence: The hook imports both domain/data concerns (`useDeleteGeneration`, `useProject`) and UI contract type (`DeleteGenerationConfirmDialogProps`) in one module (`src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx:2-4`).
Returned API is presentation-coupled (`confirmDialogProps`) rather than a domain action contract (`src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx:61-71`).

Test still mocks a different dialog module (`@/shared/components/dialogs/ConfirmDialog`) than the hook type import target (`DeleteGenerationConfirmDialog`), showing unstable layering contracts (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.test.ts:15-17`).
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1) split into `domains/generation/hooks/useDeleteGenerationAction` (mutation + project checks) and a UI adapter near dialog components (maps action state to dialog props); Stage 2) keep a temporary compatibility export in current path while updating importers; Stage 3) delete compatibility adapter after migration. Import update and validation commands: `rg -n "useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm|confirmDialogProps" src`, migrate tool/gallery callers first, then run `npx vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.test.ts` and `npx tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: `useDerivedItems` in `shared/hooks` owns direct Supabase querying and domain normalization, which should live under generation domain data/repository boundaries.
evidence: The hook file directly queries Supabase tables (`generations`, `generation_variants`) and performs merging/sorting transformations in the same module (`src/shared/hooks/useDerivedItems.ts:63-167`).
It also imports generation-domain utilities (`calculateDerivedCountsSafe`, `expandShotData`) while remaining in generic `shared/hooks` (`src/shared/hooks/useDerivedItems.ts:18-19`).

Test mocks old surfaces (`@/shared/lib/errorHandler`, `@/shared/lib/queryKeys`) that no longer match the file’s current dependency organization (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDerivedItems.test.ts:78-89` vs implementation imports at lines 16-17), indicating architecture contract drift.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1) extract `fetchDerivedItems` into a generation repository module (for example `src/domains/generation/repository/derivedItemsRepository.ts`); Stage 2) keep a thin domain hook in `src/domains/generation/hooks/useDerivedItems.ts` that only composes query keys + polling config; Stage 3) convert `src/shared/hooks/useDerivedItems.ts` to temporary re-export and remove after consumers move to domain path. Import update and validation commands: `rg -n "@/shared/hooks/useDerivedItems" src`, migrate media-lightbox and other consumers, then run `npx vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDerivedItems.test.ts` and `npx tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Single-file drag-drop upload helpers are duplicated across `shared/lib` and `shared/hooks`, causing ambiguous ownership and parallel APIs.
evidence: `src/shared/lib/dragDropUpload.ts` exports `preventDefaultDragOver` and `createSingleFileDropHandler` (`lines 4, 13`), while the same helper names and behavior exist in `src/shared/hooks/useFileDragTracking.ts` (outside this batch).

Assigned files show drag-drop concerns split by arbitrary file naming (`dragDrop.ts` for generation DnD and `dragDropUpload.ts` for file upload DnD) under a flat `shared/lib` folder already containing 70 mixed files.

Coupling evidence shows heavy consumers from both hooks and tool UI directories into `src/shared/lib/` (`directory_profiles.imported_by_dirs`: `src/shared/hooks/` 50; multiple travel-between-images directories 11-16), increasing blast radius when drag-drop APIs diverge.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1) create a dedicated package such as `src/shared/drag-drop/` with `generationDrag.ts` and `fileUploadDrop.ts`; Stage 2) move `src/shared/lib/dragDrop.ts` and `src/shared/lib/dragDropUpload.ts` into that package and de-duplicate the overlapping hook-side helpers to one implementation; Stage 3) add temporary re-export shims in old paths and remove after migration. Import update and validation commands: `rg -n "shared/lib/dragDrop|shared/lib/dragDropUpload|useFileDragTracking" src`, update imports, run `npx vitest src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDropUpload.test.ts` and `npx tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Compatibility facades keep legacy and canonical module contracts active in parallel.
evidence: `handleError.ts` is a pure pass-through compatibility wrapper while comments instruct consumers to use different reporters.
`client.ts` permits both startup bootstrap and ad-hoc lazy bootstrap (`getOrInitialize...`) instead of enforcing one initialization contract.
`button.tsx` and `cn.ts` are pass-through re-export facades, adding additional public import paths over implementation packages.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical entrypoint per subsystem and enforce it in stages: (1) add `@deprecated` JSDoc and telemetry tags to compatibility facades, (2) codemod imports to canonical modules (`presentationReporter`, `ui/theme/button`, `ui/primitives/cn`, runtime-only supabase accessor policy), (3) add lint rules to block new imports from deprecated facades, (4) remove facades after one release window with changelog notice.

findings.summary: Governance README framing does not define runtime scope or sunset policy for compatibility layers.
evidence: README links governance contracts but does not state which APIs are transitional vs stable public surface.
`handleError.ts` explicitly labels itself backward-compatible while remaining importable as normal API.
`client.ts` keeps lazy init fallback behavior, but README governance section does not describe this as allowed exception or migration target.
findings.suggestion: Add a "Contract Status" matrix in README listing each public facade (file, canonical replacement, deprecation state, removal target date/version) and align module headers to that matrix so runtime and docs share a single policy source.

findings.summary: High-fan-in contract entrypoints lack explicit governance-focused test gates.
evidence: README does not provide required quality-gate commands for contract/facade validation in CI.
`client.ts` includes branchy contract behavior (lazy init warning path) with no nearby explicit contract test file reference.
Facade files (`handleError.ts`, `button.tsx`, `cn.ts`) are thin wrappers, so regressions can ship silently unless contract tests target those seams directly.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a governance test gate: add contract tests for facade invariants (export shape, behavior parity, lazy-init policy) and document required commands in README (for example: `npm run test:contracts`, `npm run test:arch`, plus CI enforcement to fail on facade drift).

findings.summary: UI package mixes facade entrypoints with implementation-level files in one overloaded directory.
evidence: `button.tsx` and `cn.ts` are public passthrough facades in the same directory as many implementation components/utilities.
Directory profile for `src/shared/components/ui/` shows 60 files with mixed responsibilities, reducing boundary clarity.
`cn.ts` logically belongs to primitive utilities but is surfaced at top-level package without a dedicated public-entrypoint folder convention.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create `src/shared/components/ui/public/` as canonical facade folder; 2) move `button.tsx` and `cn.ts` there first (`git mv src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx src/shared/components/ui/public/button.tsx` and same for `cn.ts`); 3) add `src/shared/components/ui/index.ts` to re-export only approved public APIs; 4) update imports with `rg -l "@/shared/components/ui/(button|cn)" src | xargs sed -i '' 's|@/shared/components/ui/button|@/shared/components/ui/public/button|g; s|@/shared/components/ui/cn|@/shared/components/ui/public/cn|g'`; 5) validate with `npm run typecheck && npm run test` and architecture checks (`desloppify scan --path .`).

findings.summary: Unused helper contains infinite self-recursion and cannot terminate if called.
evidence: `buildInlineEditStateModels` calls `buildInlineEditStateModels(...)` with the same arguments (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:260-277`).
No test exercises this helper directly; current tests validate only public hook shape (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/__tests__/useInlineEditState.test.ts:223-236`).
findings.suggestion: Delete this dead helper if obsolete, or replace the self-call with actual object assembly logic and add a unit test that invokes the helper path to prevent recursion regressions.

findings.summary: Hook contract says shared queue is preserved, coordinator clears it unconditionally.
evidence: Hook comment states queue should not be cleared on unmount because other components may use it (`useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts:92-93`).
Coordinator clears queue at schedule start (`adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.ts:101`) and in cleanup (`:123-126`), contradicting shared-usage semantics.
findings.suggestion: Define one ownership model explicitly: either per-request queue handles (no global clear) or single-owner queue management. Update both code and comments to the same contract.

findings.summary: Generation completion path uses `unknown` types while dereferencing object fields.
evidence: `GenerationCreationOutcome` and `handleRegeneration` keep generation payloads as `unknown` (`generation.ts:39-41`, `:301`) but then read `.id` fields (`:308`, `:321`, `:332`).
Adjacent orchestration code defines explicit minimal interfaces (`orchestrator.ts:23-35`), showing this module has already adopted typed shape contracts elsewhere.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a minimal `GenerationRef { id: string }` (or stronger row type) and replace `unknown` in routing/regeneration signatures so property access is statically enforced.

findings.summary: Runtime state is initialized implicitly via imports/accessors instead of explicit bootstrap.
evidence: Module import instantiates `dataFreshnessManager` singleton and writes to `window.__DATA_FRESHNESS_MANAGER__` in module scope (`DataFreshnessManager.ts:343-348`).
`getSupabaseClient()` performs lazy runtime initialization when not already initialized (`client.ts:20-23`), making initialization dependent on first caller timing.
findings.suggestion: Move both services behind an explicit app bootstrap phase and keep accessors pure (fail-fast or require injected initialized instances) to remove boot-order ambiguity.

findings.summary: Error helper names do not clearly distinguish reporting vs value-carrying behavior.
evidence: `handleError` name suggests side-effect handling, but function returns `AppError` (`handleError.ts:18-20`).
`reportRecoverableError` invokes `handleError` and discards return (`recoverableError.ts:16-22`), while other paths throw returned value (`useGenerationMutations.ts:250-253`).
findings.suggestion: Split naming by contract: e.g., `normalizeAndPresentError` (returns `AppError`) vs `reportRecoverableError` (void), and migrate call sites to one semantic style per function.
